original_hypothesis.title: Timelock Admin Role Enables Instant Execution Bypassing Governance Delay
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: TaikoTimelockController.getMinDelay() returns 0 for holders of TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE. Deployment script grants this role to securityCouncil. If the securityCouncil key is compromised, an attacker can instantly execute any timelock operation (including contract upgrades, address manager changes, and token burns) without the 7-day delay.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The timelock's purpose is to give users time to exit before potentially malicious changes take effect. Returning 0 delay for admin completely negates this safety mechanism. AddressManager ownership is transferred to timelock, so instant execution could replace critical protocol addresses.
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: DeployOnL1.s.sol: _timelock.grantRole(_timelock.TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE(), securityCouncil); TaikoTimelockController.sol:getMinDelay returns 0 for TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE
preconditions: securityCouncil also has PROPOSER_ROLE and EXECUTOR_ROLE per deployment script

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: getMinDelay() returns 0 for admin, bypassing the 7-day delay
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: schedule() + execute() in same block with delay=0: replace bridge, signal_service, or taiko_token addresses
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Complete protocol takeover: redirect bridge to attacker contract, drain all bridged assets

economic_questions: What total value is controlled by contracts under timelock authority?
Can the securityCouncil key compromise scenario be mitigated?
Is the 0-delay for admin intentional design or an oversight?
validation_requirements: Confirm securityCouncil has TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE in deployment
Verify schedule+execute with 0 delay works for critical operations
Test address replacement attack via AddressManager

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: None for exploit; large for extraction

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All protocol users and bridge depositors

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
